about: Backend-neutral scoped actuator grant example (issue #33). A loop actuator returns 'grant_request' instead of holding a raw token; the controller resolves it through a GrantBroker. No secrets appear anywhere in this file by design.

policy.comment: Shape a StaticPolicyGrantBroker(allowed_scope=..., max_ttl_seconds=..., allowed_side_effect_classes=...). Real backends authenticate here; the bundled default does not.

actuator_grant_request.comment: Returned by an actuator from loop_run as action['grant_request']. Credential-free: authority lives in scope + expiry, never in a token or cookie.
actuator_grant_request.subject: work-context-abc
actuator_grant_request.reason: launch a managed agent session to drive the issue

issued_grant_handle.comment: Shape of the persistable handle inside the issued SessionGrant. Persist this (e.g. via FileGrantStore) and re-validate it after a restart to resume status checks. handle_ref is a revocable, scope-bound backend reference, NOT a reusable browser cookie or shell token.
